claim: YOLO demand evidence system already implements scope distinction (project | external | user | unknown) in schema, protocols, readiness contract, and tests.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA_DEFINITION defines evidence structure with explicit scope field requirements.
evidence.why: This is the core schema that mandates evidence scope declaration and validates project facts require project-scoped evidence.

evidence.summary: VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES is a Set containing only project, external, user, and unknown as valid scope values.
evidence.why: This enforces that evidence records can only use these four explicit scope categories.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS defines explorer, cross-checker, and verifier roles with explicit writes_code=false and result_schema.
evidence.why: This protocol definition mandates that evidence agents do not modify target project files and return results using the evidence result schema.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope function classifies evidence records by inferring scope from path, url, source field values, and explicit scope declarations.
evidence.why: This function implements the scope detection logic that routes evidence to project, external, user, or unknown categories.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordHasDeclaredScope checks if a record explicitly declares a valid scope from the VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES set.
evidence.why: This validation enforces that all evidence records must explicitly declare one of the four valid scopes.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordHasProjectLocator verifies project-scope evidence has a repo-relative path or file locator, not a URL.
evidence.why: This enforces the requirement that project facts must include concrete project file paths.

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues generates EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED blocker when evidence records lack valid scope declarations.
evidence.why: This is the readiness enforcement that blocks PRD readiness when evidence scope is not explicitly declared.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues generates PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED or PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE blockers when project facts lack project-scoped evidence.
evidence.why: This enforces that existing-project claims must be backed by project-scoped evidence with concrete file locators.

evidence.summary: Test external-only evidence cannot satisfy existing project facts and generates PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE blocker.
evidence.why: This test validates that external evidence alone cannot prove existing-project facts.

evidence.summary: Test project evidence requires a concrete project locator and generates PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED blocker when path is missing.
evidence.why: This test validates that project-scope evidence must include a repo-relative path.

evidence.summary: Test external URLs cannot be relabeled as project evidence and generates PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED blocker.
evidence.why: This test validates that URLs cannot be used as project file locators even if labeled as project scope.

evidence.summary: Test evidence records must declare valid scope explicitly and generates EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED blocker when scope field is missing.
evidence.why: This test validates that the scope field is mandatory on all evidence records.

evidence.summary: Test non-missing status notes like no missing data identified do not block readiness, distinguishing status from actual missing data.
evidence.why: This test validates the distinction between status notes and actionable missing items.

evidence.summary: Test boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass, generating BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker.
evidence.why: This test validates the evidence dispatch boundary enforcement that prevents target project file modifications.

evidence.summary: Test git-backed boundary detects untracked and gitignored project mutations and reports boundary violations.
evidence.why: This test validates boundary detection using git state to identify unauthorized project file changes.

evidence.summary: Test agent evidence without explicit scope is blocked during normalization with EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED error.
evidence.why: This test validates that the dispatch runtime enforces scope declaration on agent results.

evidence.summary: Test agent conflict remains a readiness blocker after dispatch with EVIDENCE_AGENT_CONFLICT code.
evidence.why: This test validates that evidence agent conflicts are preserved as blockers instead of being averaged away.

evidence.summary: Doctrine states evidence records must declare scope: project | external | user | unknown, and project evidence requires repo-relative path or file locator.
evidence.why: This is the product documentation that defines the scope distinction requirement.

evidence.summary: Doctrine defines the three evidence agent roles (explorer, cross-checker, verifier) and their protocols with writes_code=false.
evidence.why: This documentation explains the evidence agent contract and read-only boundary.

evidence.summary: Doctrine explains demand router, evidence policies (none | single_agent | cross_check), and PRD readiness requirements.
evidence.why: This documentation shows how scope validation integrates with the overall demand flow.

assumptions: The current git working tree status is dirty (as shown in git status at session start), which is consistent with the demand statement that YOLO demand dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested.
The stress probes mentioned in scope_out have been implemented based on the evidence dispatch test coverage shown.
risks: If the evidence scope validation logic in evidenceRecordScope function has edge cases, certain evidence patterns might be misclassified.
If boundary enforcement has false positives (e.g., gitignored files), legitimate evidence-gathering operations could be blocked.
If stress probes have not been run against real Claude -p agents, there may be integration gaps not covered by unit tests.

result.notes: Core claim verified: the codebase already implements scope distinction in schema, protocols, tests, and documentation. All four scope categories (project, external, user, unknown) are explicitly defined and validated. The readiness contract enforces scope declaration and project locator requirements. Tests cover the main validation paths including external-only evidence, missing paths, URL relabeling, and scope-less records.
